data.title: Polygon was not affected by the rsETH vulnerability.
data.description: According to official sources, the Polygon team has been actively monitoring the rsETH vulnerability: Polygon Chain, Agglayer, and the broader ecosystem, including Katana and Vaultbridge, have not been affected by this incident.

data.title: Shenzhen Stock Exchange Adjusts Hong Kong Stock Connect Securities List
data.description: The Shenzhen Stock Exchange has announced an adjustment to the list of securities eligible for trading through the Hong Kong Stock Connect. According to Jin10, the adjustment will take effect from April 20, 2026, and will include the addition of Nobikan to the list.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Indicates Bitcoin Dominance
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index currently stands at 38. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates the 90-day performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins and wrapped coins, in comparison to Bitcoin. A lower index value suggests that Bitcoin is outperforming a majority of altcoins in the market.

data.title: Iran Briefly Reopens and Closes Strait of Hormuz, Causing Vessel Turnbacks
data.description: A report from the UK-based maritime analytics company Windward reveals that Iran briefly reopened the Strait of Hormuz before closing it again, leading to 35 vessels turning back within the past 36 hours. According to Odaily, Iran announced the reopening of the strait on the 17th, prompting cautious reactions from ships. On the 18th, initial sparse traffic through the strait quickly increased as vessels rushed to pass before conditions worsened. A total of 35 ships navigated the strait that day, with 8 entering, including 4 oil tankers, 2 bulk carriers, and 2 other cargo ships. Meanwhile, 27 vessels exited, comprising 8 oil tankers, 3 bulk carriers, 15 other cargo ships, and 1 passenger ship.

data.title: Kelp DAO Faces Three Potential Solutions in rsETH Incident
data.description: Kelp DAO is currently evaluating three potential solutions to address the rsETH incident, according to an analysis by DefiLlama founder 0xngmi on the X platform. According to PANews, the first option involves distributing the losses among all users, resulting in an 18.5% devaluation for each user. The second option would concentrate the losses on rsETH holders in the L2 market, with Aave using its treasury to assist mainnet users while abandoning the L2 market. The third option suggests using a pre-attack snapshot to return assets to holders, compensating only for the portion borrowed by the hacker, although this approach is highly complex.

data.title: Maritime analysis company: 35 ships turned back while exiting the Strait of Hormuz in the past 36 hours.
data.description: A report released on the 19th by Wenward, a UK-based maritime analysis firm, stated that 35 ships turned back from their attempts to exit the Strait of Hormuz in the past 36 hours after Iran briefly reopened it and then closed it again. The report said that after Iran announced the reopening of the strait on the 17th, ships reacted cautiously that day. On the 18th, traffic was initially sparse but quickly increased as ships rushed to pass before the situation worsened. A total of 35 ships passed through the strait that day: 8 entered (4 oil tankers, 2 bulk carriers, and 2 other cargo ships); and 27 exited (8 oil tankers, 3 bulk carriers, 15 other cargo ships, and 1 passenger ship). (Xinhua)

data.title: Fed's Balance Sheet Reduction Guide Challenges Conventional Views
data.description: On April 20, Jin10 reported that a research paper titled 'User Guide to Reducing the Federal Reserve's Balance Sheet' was recently published by Fed Governor Milan and three other economists. According to Jin10, the structure of this paper bears similarities to the previously discussed 'User Guide to Restructuring the Global Trade System.' The paper challenges the conventional view that the Federal Reserve faces difficulties in significantly reducing its balance sheet. It argues that reserve requirements are largely determined by the regulatory environment and suggests that adjustments to the regulatory framework, suppression of precautionary motives, and other sources of reserve demand could enable balance sheet reduction without causing unexpected market pressure. The potential reduction space, estimated using Monte Carlo simulations, ranges from $1.2 trillion to $2.1 trillion. The report suggests that the 'balance sheet reduction guide' has some practical feasibility, although certain options may be overly idealistic.

data.title: South Korea May Join Multinational Naval Coalition Post-Iran Conflict
data.description: South Korea is considering deploying its existing anti-piracy unit in the Middle East to join a multinational naval coalition following the end of the Iran conflict. According to Odaily, this move aims to ensure the freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: Aave saw an outflow of $10.1 billion, bringing total deposits down to $35.7 billion.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Aave has experienced a continuous outflow of funds since the incident early yesterday morning, totaling $10.1 billion, reducing total deposits from $45.8 billion to $35.7 billion, of which $4.5 billion is in stablecoins. This outflow caused the stablecoin deposit rate on Aave to remain at 13.4% APY for a full day.

data.title: Following the end of the Iran-Iraq War, South Korea may deploy a contingent of its existing forces to ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Sources say that after the war with Iran, South Korea may send an existing Middle Eastern anti-piracy force to join a multinational naval alliance to ensure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. (JoongAng Ilbo, South Korea)

data.title: Key Economic Events Scheduled for Late April
data.description: Four significant macroeconomic events are anticipated in the fourth week of April. According to NS3.AI, these include China's Loan Prime Rate announcement, a speech by Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller, Tesla's earnings report, and the release of U.S. weekly initial jobless claims data.

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Open Lower as Weekend Spot Market Influences Prices
data.description: CME Bitcoin futures began trading at $74,600, marking a decrease of $2,945 from the previous trading day's closing price of $77,545. According to NS3.AI, this gap is attributed to movements in the spot market over the weekend, as CME futures do not operate during this period.

data.title: Hecto Group Invests $2.2 Million in Hashed Ventures' Third Fund
data.description: Hecto Group affiliates, Hecto Innovation and Hecto Financial, have invested 3 billion won ($2.2 million) in Hashed Ventures' third venture fund. According to NS3.AI, this investment is part of Hecto Group's strategic plan to integrate Hashed's global Web3 ecosystem with its own financial and payment infrastructure.

data.title: Crypto Fear & Greed Index Remains Neutral Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Crypto Fear & Greed Index has decreased by seven points, settling at 50, indicating a neutral sentiment in the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, the index operates on a scale from 0 to 100, incorporating various factors such as price movements of the top 10 cryptocurrencies, market volatility, derivatives data, the Stablecoin Supply Ratio, and search data from CoinMarketCap. This neutral position suggests that investors are neither overly optimistic nor pessimistic about the current market conditions.

data.title: Kelp DAO hack triggered Aave liquidity crisis, leading to $6.2 billion in user withdrawals.
data.description: The LayerZero cross-chain bridge associated with Kelp DAO was hacked on Saturday, resulting in 116,500 rsETH, worth $291 million, flowing to new wallets. The hackers used the illegally obtained rsETH as collateral for lending on Aave, causing the Aave core lending pool to reach 100% utilization and triggering a liquidity crunch. According to 0xngmi monitoring, as of early Sunday, Aave had seen a net withdrawal of $6.2 billion. Kelp DAO has suspended rsETH contracts on the Ethereum mainnet and several L2 networks. As a result, the Aave token price fell 16% to $90.13, and the Ethereum price fell 2% to $2300. Justin Sun has posted on the X platform attempting to negotiate with the hackers.

data.title: Orca Responds to Vercel Security Incident
data.description: Orca announced on the X platform that its frontend is hosted on the cloud platform Vercel. According to Odaily, in response to a security incident at Vercel, Orca has proactively rotated all potentially compromised keys and deployment credentials as a precautionary measure. The on-chain protocol and user funds remain unaffected. The company will continue to monitor the situation and provide updates as more information becomes available.

data.title: EtherFi Assures Users of Liquid Vault Security Amid Kelp rsETH Incident
data.description: EtherFi has announced on the X platform that the recent Kelp rsETH incident has not impacted its Liquid Vault. According to Odaily, users of the protocol's liquidity vault will not experience any withdrawals. Further details are expected to be released, and the funds remain secure.

data.title: Whale Opens Leveraged Positions in Brent Oil and SOL
data.description:
On April 20, a significant transaction was detected involving a large cryptocurrency holder. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, this whale deposited $5.99 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a long position in Brent Oil (BRENTOIL) using three times leverage, with the position valued at $7.5 million.

Additionally, the whale holds a short position in 254,771 SOL, also using three times leverage, with the position valued at $21 million. Currently, this position is experiencing an unrealized loss exceeding $1.679 million.

data.title: A multisignature address associated with the initial RAVE allocation transferred 23 million RAVE tokens, worth $23 million, to Bitget.
data.description: According to ZachXBT monitoring, three hours ago, the multisignature address (0x53d7) associated with the initial RAVE allocation sent approximately 23 million RAVE tokens, worth $23 million, to two Bitget deposit addresses. The price of RAVE subsequently fell from $1 to $0.60.

data.title: A whale deposited $5.99 million to open a long position in crude oil.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited $5.99 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a 3x leveraged long position in BRENTOIL, while simultaneously holding a 3x leveraged short position in SOL of 254,771, resulting in a floating loss of $1.679 million.
